creator: asami@cs.berkeley.edu
text:
/usr/bin/make can't handle the continuation line correctly
	when \ is used to break a long .elif line into two.

Fix: 

No idea.
How-To-Repeat: 
bash$ cat Makefile1
all:
.if defined(foo)
	@true
.elif !defined(foo) && !defined(foo)
	@echo ok
.else
	@echo not ok
.endif
bash$ make -f Makefile1
ok
bash$ cat Makefile2
all:
.if defined(foo)
	@true
.else
.if !defined(foo) && \
	!defined(foo)
	@echo ok
.else
	@echo not ok
.endif
.endif
bash$ make -f Makefile2
ok
bash$ cat Makefile3
all:
.if defined(foo)
	@true
.elif !defined(foo) && \
	!defined(foo)
	@echo ok
.else
	@echo not ok
.endif
bash$ make -f Makefile3
not ok                        <<<<<<<============
bash$
